headline: Sabah Fishermen Demand 12 Nautical Mile Exclusive Zone
context: Sabah Fishermen Association representing 28,000 artisanal fishers petitioned for 12-mile exclusive zone banning commercial trawlers near shore.

cards.big: 28,000 Sabah fishermen demand 12-mile exclusive zone against trawlers
cards.sub: Commercial trawlers within 5 miles of shore depleted fish stocks 60%

cards.big: Artisanal catch per trip dropped from 120kg in 2015 to 45kg in 2025
cards.sub: Small-scale fishermen income fell below RM1,500 monthly for 65% surveyed

cards.big: Fisheries Department recorded 480 trawler intrusions within 3 miles in 2025
cards.sub: Only 35 enforcement actions taken due to limited patrol vessel availability

cards.big: Indonesia and Philippines enforce 12-mile exclusive artisanal zones
cards.sub: Malaysia current 5-mile zone is smallest protection among ASEAN fishing nations

cards.big: Fishing zone boundaries test whether artisanal livelihoods are protected from industrial competition
12 nautical miles would protect fishermen's livelihoods from industrial trawlers destroying coastal stocks.
